title: Clinopodium umbrosum
taxonomy: Clinopodium umbrosum is classified as a species within the genus Clinopodium, which is a part of the mint family (Lamiaceae). The genus Clinopodium is further classified under the family Lamiaceae and the order Lamiales. Clinopodium umbrosum belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. It is also known to be a member of the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. The classification of Clinopodium umbrosum reflects its close relationship with other plants within the mint family.
uses: Clinopodium umbrosum is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and fever. The plant contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. In some parts of the world, Clinopodium umbrosum is used to treat colds and respiratory infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.

intro: Clinopodium umbrosum, also known as wood betony or creeping betony, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Clinopodium umbrosum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being combined with other herbs in preparations such as salves and ointments for topical use.
